summary: Crisp soundboard capture of the long 1989 Frost Amphitheatre show with strong performances of “He’s Gone” and a hellacious “Space > The Other One”, but marred by a channel‑swap issue and a weak “Picasso Moon”.
review: This SBD recording is generally clear and bright, delivering the power of Jerry’s lead on “He’s Gone” and an exciting “Space > The Other One” sequence. Reviewers praise the overall sound quality and the show’s length, though some note a left/right channel reversal and criticize the lackluster “Picasso Moon”. Overall it’s a solid but not flawless representation of the night.

ai_rating.rationale: Most reviewers give 4‑star marks and highlight strong musical moments, while a few point out recording quirks and a weak performance of Picasso Moon; the weighted average falls in the good‑to‑great range.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Crisp, clear vocals and instrument separation; slightly bright/high‑end tone. One reviewer notes a left/right channel reversal (keyboards on the wrong side) that can be distracting. Another mentions speaker imbalance in competing sources but favors this board for streaming.

show_quality.setlist_flow: A very long, varied set that keeps the energy high; the second half builds into a powerful “Space > The Other One” jam. Some songs (e.g., Picasso Moon) are perceived as weaker, but overall pacing is engaging.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Described as feeling the music deeply on "He's Gone" and delivering strong lead work throughout.
band_member_comments.Phil: Less prominent on this recording than usual; a reviewer noted he’s not as forward in the mix.
band_member_comments.Keys: Channel reversal puts keyboards on the opposite side, which some listeners find intolerable.
